target.name: Phoenix Tailings
target.founder: Nick Myers (CEO), Tomás Villalón (CTO, Activate Boston fellow), Anthony Balladon

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $118M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Phoenix Tailings is a Series B-stage energy and climate technology company founded by Nick Myers (CEO) and Tomás Villalón (CTO, Activate Boston fellow). The company has raised $117.8M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of direct public comparables and identified private peer benchmarks in the frontier energy sector.

Valuation was derived using stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $118M total capital raised proxy. This approach yielded an implied valuation of $224M. The stage-progression method is applicable where capital efficiency and market reception provide reliable signals, though its reliance on a single multiplier introduces bounded variance rather than triangulation across multiple methodologies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $190M to $257M, centered at $224M. The range reflects reasonable parameter sensitivity around the step-up multiplier. Key uncertainties include the absence of sector-specific comparable company data, which would refine the applicable multiplier and anchor the valuation to peer market outcomes. Revenue trajectory, capital efficiency metrics, and technical risk factors remain undocumented and materially constrain conviction in placement within this range. A more robust valuation would require additional method triangulation or expanded comparable dataset.
